questionHtml: Explain the mechanism of the reaction between 1-bromopropane, CH3CH2CH2Br, and aqueous sodium hydroxide, NaOH (aq), using curly arrows to represent the movement of electron pairs.
markschemeHtml:
curly arrow going from lone pair/negative charge on O in HO– to C ✔

curly arrow showing Br breaking ✔

representation of transition state showing negative charge, square brackets and partial bonds ✔

formation of organic product CH3CH2CH2OH AND Br– ✔

 

Do not allow curly arrow originating on H in HO–.

Accept curly arrow either going from bond between C and Br to Br in 1-bromopropane or in the transition
state.

Do not penalize if HO and Br are not at 180° to each other.

Award [3 max] for SN1 mechanism.

meta.title: Explain the mechanism of the reaction between 1-bromopropane, CH3CH2CH2Br, and aqueous sodium hydroxide, NaOH (aq), using curly arrows to represent the movement of electron pairs.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.4.12—The relative stability of carbocations in the addition reactions between hydrogen halides and unsymmetrical alkenes can be used to explain the reaction mechanism. Predict and explain the major product of a reaction between an unsymmetrical alkene and a hydrogen halide or water.
